dependencies: Third-party service providers for execution and delivery of services
Cloud-based solutions for data transmission and storage
External service providers for technology and disaster contingency support
risks: Failure of third-party service providers to properly perform processes or service interruptions
Reliance on third-party intermediaries for distribution channels
Cybersecurity risks from third-party service providers
Disruptions of infrastructure and third-party services such as communications, power, cloud services, transfer agent, investment management, trading, and accounting systems

regulatory_risks: Extensive federal, state, and foreign laws and regulations subject to change
SEC regulations on investment advisers under Investment Advisers Act of 1940
ERISA regulations for retirement savings activities
EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) compliance
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) compliance
Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) regulations limiting use of derivatives
Changes to distribution fee regulations and enhanced disclosure requirements
Privacy, data collection, cybersecurity, and data protection laws across multiple jurisdictions
SEC cybersecurity disclosure rules and amended Regulation S-P
Proposed SEC rules on predictive data analytics and AI technology
Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act requirements
European Market Infrastructure Regulation (EMIR)
EU Sustainable Finance Disclosure Regulation (SFDR) for ESG disclosures
EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive
California Climate Accountability Package
Proposed SEC climate disclosure rules
State legislation restricting business with entities considering ESG factors
Retirement Security Rule expanding ERISA fiduciary definition
Financial transaction tax proposals in US and EU
Changes to tax-deferred investment options and capital gains tax rates
FINRA regulations for broker-dealer activities
Net capital requirements from federal, state, and international regulatory agencies
Pillar Two Model Rules introducing global 15% minimum tax

sanction_risks: Economic, trade and sanctions laws and regulations exposure
Risks from political or diplomatic developments, government policies, wars, conflicts or civil unrest (Russian invasion of Ukraine, Israel-Hamas war)
Trade wars or tariffs including those imposed or threatened by the U.S.
Potential nationalization of property in countries outside the U.S.
